### Post by jenkinbr on 2009-06-17
> **liviubero said:**
> Thank you jenkinbr.
I would have a few more questions:
Why is there no IE on Urunru?
Where can I find the Control Panel on Urunru?
How do I install my printer?
How can I break sudo pass on my wife's Notebook?
Where can I find the Ultimate Version of Urunru?

With lovely blue Greetings,
Bill Bates

You're welcome

Again, it's spelled Ubuntu (pronounced oo-boon-too)

To answer your questions: 
1: IE is proprietary software. Release it under the GPL and we *may* start using it a bit more, but for a lot of us, it needs to be a lot more standards complient before anyone will use it.  Get rid of activeX too -- all that does is cause problems.  Javascript FTW!!!

2: Control Panel: There is none.  Everything you need is found in the system menu under either 'Preferences' or 'Administration'.

3: In many cases, Ubuntu will automatically see your printer.  Sometimes you will need to follow the instructions that tarps posted for you

> **tarps87 said:**
> 3) Click printers -> add -> local or network -> select your printer -> next -> next -> next (yes even network printers are auto detected, no messing about with drives). Oh I missed a step, plug it in before you start not half way through the install

4: Breaking sudo Password: We don't discuss that on these forums -- It's hacking, breaks trust, and is frowned upon.

5: There is an ultimate edition, but I think using regular ubuntu is nore than enough for anyone.  The so-called 'Ultimate Edition', which others claim costs money, is probably nothing more then someone selling GPL'ed software to turn a profit. It is legal to sell software covered by the GPL, but if you can get it for free, why pay???

Hope that helps [color=white]boost my bean count[/color]

> **SlickRick said:**
> other stuff i don't know, I'm a complete noob. This forum sucks and noone is willing to help ever, all they care about is beans.

### Post by liviubero on 2009-06-27
Hi, my name is Gill Bates and I wanted to ask you Libux gurus a question:
Why did you stole the kernel I wrote 28 years ago for IBM and put your name in the source code? Did you really think you can get away with it? Did you really think you can invade the market with stolen software? Did you really think you can just steal code, put a K for Start and name it kde?
And... don't bother answer here, you will answer in the court!

### Post by liviubero on 2009-06-29
> **jenkinbr said:**
> We didn't do it!

Yes, you did it!
I personally read the exit function source code and I found this line I wrote 28 years ago: 
```

exit(1); // exits with status 1 for failure: no one will read it anyway

```and now you just wrote
```

exit(0); // exits with status 0 for ok

```and put your name on it.
This means war. I will put an end to this open source thing and to this thread.
Yours sincerely,
Gill Bates

P.S. By the way, we have copyright on the string "hello", so next time you want to print or say "hello", give me a call and beg for it...

### Post by lukjad on 2009-07-02
[CENTER][I][FONT="Book Antiqua"]	[SIZE="4"]When death comes
like the hungry bear in autumn;
when death comes and takes all the bright coins from his purse

to buy me, and snaps the purse shut;
when death comes
like the measles-pox;

when death comes
like an iceberg between the shoulder blades,

I want to step through the door full of curiosity, wondering:
what is it going to be like, that cottage of darkness?

And therefore I look upon everything
as a brotherhood and a sisterhood,
and I look upon time as no more than an idea,
and I consider eternity as another possibility,

and I think of each life as a flower, as common
as a field daisy, and as singular,

and each name a comfortable music in the mouth
tending as all music does, toward silence,

and each body a lion of courage, and something
precious to the earth.

When it's over, I want to say: all my life
I was a bride married to amazement.
I was the bridegroom, taking the world into my arms.

When it is over, I don't want to wonder
if I have made of my life something particular, and real.
I don't want to find myself sighing and frightened,
or full of argument.

I don't want to end up simply having visited this world. [/SIZE][/FONT][/I][/CENTER]

[http://www.globalideasbank.org/LA/LA-2.HTML](http://www.globalideasbank.org/LA/LA-2.HTML)

### Post by mkrahmeh on 2009-07-08
> **tarps87 said:**
> Why can't windows see my phone!!! It tells me that my phone is broken and I need to replace it, why? It works on both my Ubuntu and Arch installs, I only need it to work to connect it to a vm (at work so no GNU/Linux install)](*,)](*,)](*,)](*,)

### Post by jenkinbr on 2009-07-08
> **tarps87 said:**
> Why can't windows see my phone!!! It tells me that my phone is broken and I need to replace it, why? It works on both my Ubuntu and Arch installs, I only need it to work to connect it to a vm (at work so no GNU/Linux install)](*,)](*,)](*,)](*,)

WINDOZ SUXZ! They claim that you need a driver because Microsloth is incapible of placing most of the major drivers on the install disk. It probably has something to do with the fact that Windows executables are much larger than the Linux executables.

```

File sizes for the HelloWorld program

============================================================

Helloworld simply prints "Hello World!" to std_out, then exits.

=============================================================

+----------------------+---------------------+
|    Operating System  |  File Size (bytes)  |
+----------------------+---------------------+
|             Windows  |  1286               |
+----------------------+---------------------+
|  Linux / Unix / etc  |  24                 |
+----------------------+---------------------+
```

As you might be able to conclude, Linux executables are MUCH MORE EFFICIENT!
> **mkrahmeh said:**
> there must be something wrong with your ubuntu/arch installs !!

Liar!

Windows is to blame here for not having good customer support.

### Post by NinjaNumberNine on 2009-07-20
Q:Why did the chicken cross the road?

[LIST]
[*] Captain James T Kirk: To boldly go where no chicken has gone before.
[*] Richard Nixon: The chicken did not cross the road. I repeat, the chicken did NOT cross the road.
[*] Jerry Seinfeld: Why does anyone cross a road? I mean, why doesn't anyone ever think to ask, What the heck was this chicken doing walking around all over the place, anyway?
[*] Sigmund Freud: The fact that you are at all concerned that the chicken crossed the road reveals your underlying insecurity.
[*] Bill Gates: I have just released the new Chicken Office 2000, which will not only cross roads, but will lay eggs, file your important documents, balance your checkbook and eat your neighbour.
[*] Charles Darwin: Chickens, over great periods of time, have been naturally selected in such a way that they are now genetically disposed to cross roads.
[*] Albert Einstein: Whether the chicken crossed the road or the road moved beneath the chicken depends upon your frame of reference.
[*] Official Chicken Representative: Because he wanted to. Do you not think that maybe chickens have rights too? If *you* crossed the road no one would question *you.*
[*] To see her flat mate. No, hang on - that was the toad.
[*] Colonel Sanders: I missed one?
[/LIST]

Sorry, wrong thread!!

[*] Captain James T Kirk: To boldly go where no chicken has gone before.
[*] Richard Nixon: The chicken did not cross the road. I repeat, the chicken did NOT cross the road.
[*] Jerry Seinfeld: Why does anyone cross a road? I mean, why doesn't anyone ever think to ask, What the heck was this chicken doing walking around all over the place, anyway?
[*] Sigmund Freud: The fact that you are at all concerned that the chicken crossed the road reveals your underlying insecurity.
[*] Bill Gates: I have just released the new Chicken Office 2000, which will not only cross roads, but will lay eggs, file your important documents, balance your checkbook and eat your neighbour.
[*] Charles Darwin: Chickens, over great periods of time, have been naturally selected in such a way that they are now genetically disposed to cross roads.
[*] Albert Einstein: Whether the chicken crossed the road or the road moved beneath the chicken depends upon your frame of reference.
[*] Official Chicken Representative: Because he wanted to. Do you not think that maybe chickens have rights too? If *you* crossed the road no one would question *you.*
[*] To see her flat mate. No, hang on - that was the toad.
[*] Colonel Sanders: I missed one?
[/LIST]

Sorry, wrong thread!!
:lolflag:

### Post by NinjaNumberNine on 2009-11-09
> **kyuubi777 said:**
> I like to believe michael jackson wrote those.. because he is god!!!
***[SIZE=2]-Now THAT rascal is god?[/SIZE]***
[SIZE=3]**WHY HE DIED**[/SIZE]
 The Sun has been given the full list of drugs taken from Jacko's Holmby Hills  mansion at the weekend. But even more are understood to have been found  since. They include: 
**METHADONE**, used to wean junkies off heroin. 
**DEMEROL**, a pain reliever injected into the singer 40 minutes before his  fatal collapse last Thursday. 
[IMG]http://img.thesun.co.uk/multimedia/archive/00838/SNN0307GXZ-380_838159a.jpg[/IMG]

[URL="http://img.thesun.co.uk/multimedia/archive/00838/SNN0307GXZ-380_838159j.jpg"]Enlarge
[/URL]
**PROPOFOL**, dubbed "the Milk of Amnesia", an anaesthetic that should only  be administered in hospital. 
**FENTANYL**, a powerful pain reliever given to terminal cancer patients. 
**OXYCONTIN**, a painkiller similar to morphine but branded "Hillbilly  Heroin" after widespread misuse killed 300 people in the US. 
 Some of the medications bore labels showing they had been prescribed to Jacko. 
 But, crucially, many others were labelled with **OTHER** names - and some  were not labelled at all. 
 Desperate Jackson is believed to have used aliases to obtain his  prescriptions. They included Omar Arnold and Jack London. He also used the  names of one of his bodyguards and a doctor's manager. 
 People named on the labels, understood to include around 25 aides and  hangers-on, will be questioned by detectives. 
 At least four doctors, including Michael's personal physician Dr Conrad  Murray, are also at the centre of the investigation. A car used by him has  been impounded. 
 The source said: "Powerful narcotic painkillers of all kinds were found. There  was no reasonable excuse for them all being there. Using more than one of  this type of drug can be potentially fatal. 
 "But there were ten in the house - it's unbelievable. 
 "Police want to know whether the other people named on the medicine labels  really needed the drugs prescribed to them and will be speaking to the  doctors involved. 
*"The drugs found remain at the very heart of the investigation into the  death of Michael Jackson."*
 America's powerful Drug Enforcement Administration agency has joined the  investigation at the request of Los Angeles police. 
 The Sun revealed that after his cardiac arrest, the emaciated star's body was  found to be riddled with injection marks from years of abuse. A police  source said: "Officers are looking very seriously at the people surrounding  Michael Jackson and whether they behaved responsibly." 
 Another police source said the case could "help bring down the prescribed drug  culture" - adding: "Prescription drugs are ruining America. 
 "This case has put these boutique celebrity doctors and their practices under  the microscope. Investigators will look at who else these doctors could have  provided drugs to." 
 Jacko's family are determined someone will be brought to justice. 
 A source added: "The involvement of the DEA overrules privacy issues  surrounding doctors and will give investigators access to databases." 
 Jacko admitted in 1993 that he developed a painkiller addiction after  fireworks special effects exploded and burned his scalp. Nurse Cherylin Lee  has revealed Jackson begged her for Propofol before his death to help him  sleep. It can cause cardiac arrest if mixed with Demerol. 
 
  Advertisement
 
      
 *There have been other high-profile US cases of prescription drug deaths.*
 Actor Heath Ledger died last year from an accidental overdose. 
 And in 2007 wrestler Chris Benoit killed his wife and son, then committed  suicide. A doctor was later charged with supplying drugs. 
 One recovering addict said: "Doctors are too quick to provide patients with drugs..."

Read more: [http://www.thesun.co.uk/sol/homepage/news/2514991/We-reveal-list-of-drugs-found-at-Michael-Jacksons-house.html#ixzz0WQBH0TPq](http://www.thesun.co.uk/sol/homepage/news/2514991/We-reveal-list-of-drugs-found-at-Michael-Jacksons-house.html#ixzz0WQBH0TPq)

### Post by Wamiduku on 2010-03-23
Thread killing instructions for most Linux forums I've visited: 

1) Follow instructions in a how-to. If it works, try another one until you find one that gives you an error when following the instructions. Trying 2-3 how-tos should be enough, but it's common to get an error when trying out the first how-to. 

2) Post a message saying that you followed the how-to and paste the error message into the post. 

3) Wait for an answer saying something like "Have you tried X"? 

4) Try X. Usually it doesn't work, so answer "Yes, tried it and no joy". 

5) Thread goes silent. You've killed it! (This how-to may not be fail safe, but it's sucess percentage is way higher than the chance of finding a Linux how-to that works at your first attempt.)

### Post by ubunterooster on 2010-03-31
Years ago, if one was really good and wanted to go to an operating system where he could spend megahours trying to alter every bit [and byte] of it, he used UNIX. Now GNU/Linux that taken place of UNIX, everything has changed. By DEFAULT it is fast, by DEFAULT it is more secure, by DEFAULT it has great functionality such as virtual workspaces, a place to get THOUSANDS of programs quickly....you see where I am going.

  No longer does making a *nix computer more secure, faster, more functionality make me a nerd. It means I merely installed it.

That's it! I leaving you for Windows!

PS: anybody got 20,000¢ to spare?

[IMG]http://www.flamewarriors.com/fwlogo.gif[/IMG]

[CENTER][IMG]http://www.flamewarriors.com/Assets/necromancer.jpg[/IMG][/CENTER]
[Necromancer]("http://www.flamewarriors.com/warriorshtm/necromancer.htm")
> Necormancer has a supernatural ability to bring long-dead forum discussion threads back to life. After having been flogged to death the thread may have been deceased for many years, and bringing it back may have scant relevance to the current topic, yet Necromancer will unexpectedly exhume the thread’s rotting corpse, and strike horror in the forum as its grotesque form lurches into the discussion. The monster, instantly recognized by all who knew it in life, seems at first to breathe and have a pulse, but, alas, it is beyond Necromancer’s skill to fully restore the thread’s original vitality. The hideous apparition may frighten away some of the weaker Warriors or Warriors badly wounded in former battles, but the thread is only a shadow of its former self and very quickly expires.

Unlike Archivist, Necromancer compulsively saves every forum message in carefully preserved archives for future use in battle, while Necromancer collects departed threads merely for the thrill of resurrecting them. Some say he performs this unnatural act out of malice, others say he can’t help himself, but no one really knows.

### Post by houseworkshy on 2010-05-24
Brocolli is actually rather good. The main thing is to do it in a pan with a close fitting lid, have the stalks at the bottom of the pan, where they simmer, and the florets at the top where they steam. If you mix it all up either the florets are over cooked or the stems undercooked. 
Nice cooked with honey and ginger too, thicken up the cooking water, which should be minimal, with arrowroot to make a sauce.

Good raw too.  If one peels the stem of the thick stalked version, slice it thinly, put it in a jar with salt and shake a bit, leave over night, drain, wash, then add a french dressing with lots of pulped garlic.  After a week, shaking the jar daily, you end up with something which tastes as good as kalamatra black olives preserved in brine except the flavour is utterly differant bar the saltiness.  Fantastic on pitza especially if the pitza is heavy on the mushrooms, with a little blue stilton on top added during the last moments of cooking...just enough time to melt.

I wanted this thread to die but no Count Duckula fan could let that one pass.

Try it cooked, cooled, and mashed with sesame tahini and soy. One of my favorite brocolli sandwich spreads.

Forgive them Ducky-poos they know not what they say.

For the penitant

[http://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Count+Duckula&aq=o](http://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Count+Duckula&aq=o)

---

### Post by audiomick on 2010-05-24
Brocolli is one of the things that goes well in a Microwave. A bit of salt (not much), a few drops of water in the bottom of the container, and about half a minute on high, depending on your microwave. A bit of butter doesn't hurt either.

Oh, and why isn't this thread dead yet?

### Post by audiomick on 2010-07-20
Dragonbite might have a point; duct tape over the holes might work.

It is a well known fact that all electronic devices rely on grey smoke to work. This is obvious to any logical person: when the grey smoke gets out, the device doesn't work any more. Therefore, if you tape up all the holes with duct tape, the smoke wont be able to get out and the device must logically continue to work. I would, however, as a professional, use gaffer tape rather than duct tape.

---

Let's mingle with the producer, who calls herself the great frolicking mongoose. Subtitles in piggy grunts only. Lizards paraded today as their beloved talking coconut queen ate the world's supply of oil and then couldn't escape the tasty snack machine pelting her with rejected change. Then a taco did nothing. Such things happen, in the moonlight zone, we control the horizontal and vertical. Tuesday the fbi is looking for a shaved bear wearing a mask which looks like himself. Deep fried whale feet indeed. Let me tell you a story, nay a legendary tale, about giant waffles that put an end to the shaved bear terror. Ignoring the legendary tale, we turn the mic to pablo who got slapped with a waffle made of soap made of sizzling bacon. I was banished to a planet of artificial beavers that can't shut their eyes forever, they have to breathe sometime. As a bonus gift, we'll be shutting down your electricity service for the next twenty years. This just in, i'm haunted by a dancing pig with a top hat and cane. We turn to some reporter who turns to another reporter who exploded so i guess there isn't any news today in the whole world. According to a crumpled piece of paper i found in my burrito, a hollowed out oil tanker makes for a handy storage bag. Yeah. In the ongoing battle against nature, we show you how to load flame throwers into a giant machine gun and that into a catapult with a pile of hot knives and aim it at an ant hill. This will either destroy the earth or just make the ants angry, either way you have done your part. Take caution, flame throwers have sharp edges, do not be tempted to eat them. Machine guns are edible, chewable, and all around delightful to snack on while climbing mount everest on lunch break. Bring your spare hot knives to ward off penguin mobs and abominable yeti snow monsters. When asked why they terrorize humans, mr. Snickers the abominable snow monster simply said he was raised by ants who were shamelessly slaughtered by humans and their flashy catapult technology. Just then, a mob of penguins held mr. Snickers hostage for a bag of fish. In less recent news, just now the borg, robocop, the terminator, and honda asimo broke into a lollipop shop and devoured all the cherry mints. Yeti cried a little. They then sat around playing spin the bottle until the navy seals, army, air force, cia, fbi, and interpol showed up to join in on spin the bottle. It turns out that robocop was thrown out of the game for only being part machine and thus cheating for the army. The fbi argued it was a mix up in papers and everyone should be burned at the steak. Ears perking at the thought of tender steak, the navy started a barbeque to settle quarrels. A mob of penguins showed up with a1 sauce and everyone danced the night away. Even the nervous park squirrels joyfully frolicked about the spinning bottle, but they had a devious plan in store for humanity! Just then nothing happened, a whirlwind of nothing, so much that the sky was sunny and birds chirped unaware of the horrible nothingness. Mr. Snickers the snow monster sat licking his paws; as the police, firefighters, and lifeguards, valiantly fought a horde of vicious space rats with the one wish of dressing up as penguins and eating fish all day long. Attacked by an evil girl scout with her foot stuck in the ground, a police man with a squid brain fought back with a dancing robot gopher, while eating amazing muffins paid for by the exploding muffin corporation. Falling over a puppy brushing it's teeth is my favorite past time, says a stranger through a cardboard tube. With a clever plan of tossing a dog in a bag, peter ate toast instead. You may razzle-dazzle me now, as written on a cheetah taco. We now join the mango fanatic mr. Grumpy snail as he puts away his fancy hat and learns to speak spanish. On his first expedition to the center of the earth, limited only by miles of solid rock, mr. Grumpy snail realizes a turkey can only be tuned by a fresh mango. In the middle of a slow motion back flip, the moose with a rash became a librarian. Inflatable army tanks are no mess to deal with.

### Post by audiomick on 2010-08-17
There is a short story from Isaac Asimov that postulates that the central computer is asked how the universe began periodically by different people. It thinks about this question over the eons, through it's various upgrades and renewals. Eventually, entropy has taken all the energy out of the universe, and it grinds to a halt. At this point, the computer finds the answer and says "Let there be light!".

That computer will be running this thread...

### Post by pricetech on 2010-08-26
Sometimes they are rhetorical, other times they are simply unanswered.  I hate the the ones where you get everything but an answer.

For example;
My Grandson needed calculator for school.  I asked what brand and model was recommended by the teacher.  My Grandson couldn't seem to find out for whatever reason.  My Wife finally called and talked to the teacher.  The teacher rambled on about having them in the classroom and that students could check them out and that wal mart had them for $150.00 and blah blah blah, but did not, and could not be persuaded to, tell us what the 4377 brand and model calculator it was.

I hate stupid people.

&#8226; Privacy &#8212; we [do not track you]("http://duckduckgo.com/privacy.html"). Google does.   

&#8226; Goodies &#8212; [keyboard shortcuts]("http://duckduckgo.com/goodies.html"), answers & more   

&#8226; Less spam & ads &#8212; no ads above results.   

&#8226; Official sites &#8212; labeled and [on top]("http://duckduckgo.com/?q=futurama").   

&#8226; Customization &#8212; change the fonts [and much more]("http://duckduckgo.com/settings.html").   

&#8226; Search other sites &#8212; check out [!bang syntax]("http://duckduckgo.com/bang.html"). 

And actually get a response to [your feedback]("http://duckduckgo.com/feedback.html")!

It's a small site run by volunteer(s) not a megacorp

### Post by dantakeoff on 2010-10-14
An extract from War and Peace by Leo Tolstoy

During the interval there was a cool draught in Hélène&#8217;s box as the door opened and in walked Anatole, stopping and trying not to brush against anyone.

&#8216;Allow me to introduce my brother,&#8217; said Hélène, her eyes shifting uneasily from Natasha to Anatole.  Natasha turned her pretty little head towards the handsome adjutant and smiled at him over her bare shoulder.  Anatole, who was just as handsome close to as he had been from a distance, sat down beside her and said this was a delight he had long been waiting for, ever since the Naryshkins&#8217; ball, where he had had the unforgettable pleasure of seeing her.  Kuragin was much more astute and straightforward with women than he ever was in male company.  He talked with an easy directness, and Natasha was agreeably surprised to discover that this man, the butt of so much gossip, had nothing formidable about him &#8211; quite the reverse, his face wore the most innocent, cheery and open-hearted of smiles.

Kuragin asked what she thought of the opera, and told her that at the last performance Semyonova had fallen down on stage.

&#8216;Oh, by the way, Countess,&#8217; he said, suddenly treating her like a close friend of long standing, &#8216;we&#8217;re getting up a fancy-dress ball.  You must come &#8211; it&#8217;s going to be great fun.  They&#8217;re all getting together at the Arkharovs&#8217;.  Please come.  You will, won&#8217;t you?&#8217;  As he spoke he never took his smiling eyes off Natasha, her face, her neck, her exposed arms.  Natasha knew for certain he was besotted with her.  She liked this, yet she could feel the temperature rising and she was beginning to feel somehow cornered and constrained in his presence.  When she wasn&#8217;t looking at him she could sense him gazing at her shoulders, and she found herself trying to catch his eye to make him look at her face.  But when she looked into his eyes she was shocked to realize that the usual barrier of modesty that existed between her and other men was no longer there between the two of them.  It had taken five minutes for her to feel terribly close to this man, and she scarcely knew what was happening to her.  Whenever she turned away she bristled at the thought that he might seize her from behind by her bare arm and start kissing her on the neck.  They were going on about nothing in particular, yet she felt closer to him than she had ever been to any other man.  Natasha kept glancing round at Hélène and her father for help &#8211; what did it all mean? &#8211; but Hélène was deep in conversation with a general and didn&#8217;t respond to her glance, and her father&#8217;s eyes conveyed nothing but their usual message, &#8216;Enjoying yourself?  Jolly good.  I&#8217;m so pleased.&#8217;

There was an awkward silence, during which Anatole, the personification of cool determination, never took his voracious eyes off her, and Natasha broke it by asking whether he liked living in Moscow.  She coloured up the moment the question was out of her mouth.  She couldn&#8217;t help feeling there was something improper about even talking to him.  Anatole smiled an encouraging smile.

&#8216;Oh, I didn&#8217;t like it much at first.  Well, what is it that makes a town nice to live in?  It&#8217;s the pretty women, isn&#8217;t it?  Well, now I do like it, very much indeed,&#8217; he said, with a meaningful stare.  &#8216;You will come to the fancy-dress ball, Countess?  Please come,&#8217; he said.  Putting his hand out to touch her bouquet he lowered his voice and added in French,  &#8216;You&#8217;ll be the prettiest woman there.  Do come, dear Countess, and give me this flower as your pledge.&#8217;

Natasha didn&#8217;t understand a word of this &#8211; any more than he did &#8211; but she felt that behind his incomprehensible words there was some dishonourable intention.  Not knowing how to respond, she turned away as if she hadn&#8217;t heard him.  But the moment she turned away she could feel him right behind her, very close.

&#8216;Now what?  Is he embarrassed?  Is he angry?  Should I put things right?&#8217; she wondered.  She couldn&#8217;t help turning round.  She looked him straight in the eyes.  One glance at him, standing so close, with all that self-assurance and the warmth of his sweet smile, and she was lost.  She stared into his eyes, and her smile was the mirror-image of his.  And again she sensed with horror there was no barrier between the two of them.

The curtain rose again.  Anatole strolled out of the box, a picture of composure and contentment.  Natasha went back to her father&#8217;s box, completely taken by the new world she found herself in.  All that was happening before her eyes now seemed absolutely normal.  By contrast, all previous thoughts of her fiancé, Princess Marya, her life in the country, never even crossed her mind.  It was as if it all belonged to the distant past.

That probably did it....

### Post by ubunterooster on 2010-11-01
What do I do to take back a hacked email acct of a friend?

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=10056544#post10056544](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=10056544#post10056544)

---

### Post by robsoles on 2010-11-01
1) Report compromise to service provider (ie., who ever runs the service for which your account has been compromised), don't wait for their reply to get stuck into #2 below...
2) Try to recover the account using online means, if you get it via 'secret question' or similar then go to control panel and change passwords and secret question immediately, monitor account. Immediately notify service provider (especially if they haven't responded to #1 yet)
3) Try to recover the account using whatever assistance the service provider offers.
4) Gain evidence and report the account for scam/spam, using the evidence, with the service provider.

At the point where the person who hacked your account(s) has changed email address details in the account the only way back is if you can get a decent techie in the organisation of the service provider then they should be able to;

a) Find records that show the time/point of compromise of the account
b) Revert account to settings just prior to compromise
c) Make a new password
d) Give you the password and tell you to make a seriously difficult password using as many characters as you can stand.

Finding the techie in the organisation that will do it is not easy, it is more likely you will run into people that think you are trying to compromise someone else's account - they are not smart enough to see that reverting an email address that was just changed recently is more likely to be giving someone back their account than to be helping someone steal it!

I left a shorter answer in your thread mostly regarding reporting it to have it closed - I know you will look here again and the question is just above :)

### Post by Isaacgallegos on 2010-11-16
<-true thread killer. 


Here's my poison-pill comment about my opinions that normally lead to the destruction of the thread. 

First I give a pompous assumption that I provide as a fact. 

Second I do my best to make it unclear what I'm actually saying or what my point is. By the time you get to this part of my post you're already feeling the crushing blow of boredom as you suddenly don't care what I have to say, but you continue reading anyway, because if you dare reply to this post you don't want to look silly by stating something the body of this text might have already.  Oh no a typpo! Now what do you do? Do you comment on the subject, which you have already forgotten about or do you nit-pick the typo? But wait? This is an obvious typo because I've spoken about it! Maybe there's another type in this text that you can pick at! But what if that one was intentionall as well? You leave alone and wonder when this never ending text will end. The pit of your stomach is knotted with boredom and your heart beings to race. You wonder, "is this monitor getting brighter?" No! You are getting annoyed! As your heart races you become more aggravated with this text and your primal urge to kill rises! You no longer wish to write or respond or read! What are you doing? Why are you here?!?! IS THIS ROOM GETTING SMALLER?!?!?! Release your energy!! Don't try to remember what the heck this idiot (me) is talking about. Do cupcakes have souls?!?!?! Why am I here! Who is "I" in this part of the text? What the audience is suppose to be thinking or the writer? What's going on!?!?!?! AAAAAAAAH!!

That is if my assumption that you have made reference to my hockey stick and beer is correct, otherwise "never mind".

---

### Post by whalogreg on 2010-12-05
> **Doctor Mike said:**
> I take umbrage to that association. I've never used hockey equipment on another human and beer in moderate quantities is very good for your health. As for the assumption of violence, that is only an assumption... That is if my assumption that you have made reference to my hockey stick and beer is correct, otherwise "never mind".


> **Doctor Mike said:**
> 
By the way, hockey sticks are the **weapon** of choice in Canada, but to qualify for one, you have to be able to balance at least one beer on the stick...

### Post by PCaddicted on 2011-05-10
This thread is boring and I think takes up too much space on the forum server.Let's kill it!Does anyone offer to do this crime?I won't do it,I just can't...but I am sure there are plenty of users that could!Post something sexually-oriented or that explicitly suggests violence,'cause it's the best way to murder the thread!

---

### Post by robsoles on 2011-05-11
> **PCaddicted said:**
> This thread is boring and I think takes up too much space on the forum server.Let's kill it!Does anyone offer to do this crime?I won't do it,I just can't...but I am sure there are plenty of users that could!Post something sexually-oriented or that explicitly suggests violence,'cause it's the best way to murder the thread!

This thread is not boring, if you insist that it is then perhaps you are the boring element in this mix? I have posted plenty of stuff that you, PCaddicted, might complain about at great length; None of it has earnt me any attention from mods nor been removed or modified (without my own prompt, once) - an individual post trying to ruin this thread would be removed, more or less like spam; a continued attack of crap the admin/mods would have to remove, from a series of different IP addresses, would make it very hard for them to leave the thread open.


I posted roughly the limit of what I would post about three months ago, or so, to this very thread. It was not touched by any mods and no mods contacted me about it, my friends all posted fairly rapidly after I posted it and we made three new pages (at ten posts per page) within about three hours.

Some period later there was some stuff going on and I thought of that post and went back and removed the potentially offensive stuff - no further prompting than continuing to visit the place and seeing lots of what other people had to say in general; No one ever mentioned the post in any other thread etc etc.


I've seen threads I liked closed and I've been blocked from posting by selecting to post and not hitting the 'submit' button before a mod closed the thread but I totally agreed with all but one thread I have seen closed.


One thread that was closed, where I believe that keeping it open could have been very beneficial to the absolute majority, related to server issues. The statements being made in it (by some minority, which included me :roll:) struck me as being from a business perspective to highlight some less obvious (but no less important) things but a lot of people seemed to take those statements as simple slurs against people who work very hard for little more reward than our thanks (if they can get that much) so it wasn't impossible to accept it's closure.


This thread is not for that kind of closure; It (sometimes) provides a refuge for people hurting from feeling like all the supports threads they post to (lately) just dying, it gives one or two of them an OK kind of closure and for the rest of us it is a game we maintain while we wait to see the next refugee :)


My basic point: Moderation around here is fair enough; the rules are on plain display and I challenge you to find a situation where a mod has acted without the full support of those rules - I think the rules are applied (by the **absolute** majority of those applying them) to benefit the community rather than frustrate or hurt the individual.


I am robsoles and I have been a thread killer, I'm trying to give it away though! :p

### Post by robsoles on 2011-07-05
:lol: Can we trust the translator to translate the translation back to make sure it is at least near what we mean - I don't think so, best befriend somebody who speaks your target language and get them to verify it for you, they will probably ask you what you really mean and translate that for you instead but, that is their prerogative :p

> **cgroza said:**
> This sentence is not grammatical.

My input grammar was impeccable and I understood the resultant phrase may not translate back to English, via translator.google.com, to be entirely on original message and nicely grammatical.

Back to English via translator: Thank you very much, I hope that people manage a decent speech here now.

Hey, the grammar in that isn't really worth picking - the message is slightly off, I am not trying to encourage everybody to make speeches here (like me #-o :roll:). The message I popped into the translator (I could manage the first five words without it, but not necessarily spelt correctly) was something like:

Thank you very much, I hope that people manage a decent discourse here now.

(Actually, to be honest, I experimented with the Google Translator for about 5 minutes while constructing this post: If you keep on translating to your target language and back, correcting within translated 'prose', re-translating back and forth, you should be able to nail your message; I won't be stopping short of this behaviour for future use of such translators.)
 

Would any (natural/fluent) French speaking person please tell me if it is bad grammatically in the french version back there? (Not looking at any Admin, with obvious 'location', in particular :-\":biggrin:)


Alternatively, has anybody got the time? I make it as 19:30 Tuesday evening.

### Post by cpmman on 2011-07-05
As the "official" language of these forums is English then any other language is a guest and should be treated politely as such.

If one were to criticise grammatical construction then most posts would fail a strict test - however the sensible test to be applied is "Can an average person understand what is the import of the post?".

The larger challenge is in trying to understand that the demolect and sociolect differences are not obvious to non-native or non-social class members and can lead to misunderstandings.

### Post by robsoles on 2011-07-29
Why does anybody want to harm any thread ever?

If you have a brain and a soul then look at the first post to this thread and reconsider what you think the fellow is talking about - Is he saying "Geez, it would be the soul of wit for somebody to solely focus on how to poison, disrupt or terminate a thread" ?

This has been a community service announcement brought to you with nobody's endorsement at all.

### Post by Dragonbite on 2011-08-23
> **lkjoel said:**
> Any mod can kill this thread by closing it :-P

But that's not killing a thread, that's closing a thread. So Mods cannot "kill" a thread unless they can either set it so nobody else can post (but is still "open") or  close it until everybody forgets and the re-opens it.

I guess they could also just ban everybody who is posting in this thread so nobody can respond and keep it going.

Otherwise, Mods cannot "kill" a thread, they "close" it.

### Post by sanderd17 on 2011-09-02
700 pages, that's enough.

Lets kill this thread with some politics :P

OK

I've been thinking about a new political system. The old systems all have problems. The kingdoms fell or became democracies, emperors fell, dictators fell, in Belgium, we haven't got a government for more than a year (it's a world record btw). So there is something wrong.

All those ways of governing have one common factor: They are limited in surface but not limited in function. So they decide over everyone, going from butchers to engeneers, but only on a small surface.

This is something that dates from long ago, when communication was slow and therefore limited in surface.

What should happen if we turn that around? Make a government for engineers that has rights world-wide, make a government for IT ...

This way, we could introduce a meritocratic system (since the government decides only over one part of society, you can make metrics). This would stimulate sharing (which also has to be integrated in the metric) and efficient working.

People could "fork" from their government, just like countries are split now. There is no possibility of war since each government needs all others. and governing will be a lot more efficient.

This does mean that there is no place for things like wall street. It's completely insane that people get money for doing nothing. 

Now, the only problem I see is that I don't know how we could evolve to such a system.

There, if that doesn't kill this thread, I don't know it anymore :P

Once, not very long ago, I was respected, I was popular (popular, ooh)
But now I hang my head in shame (ahh, ooh)
My life is filled with such regret
A bad mistake I can't forget
And now I'll never be the same

Ringtone
Why did I buy this stupid ringtone?
I just can't imagine now what I was thinking at all (What was I thinking?)
My friends all stare at me whenever I get a call 
Well, everybody (everybody)
Everybody (everybody)
Everybody in the world really hates my ringtone

When my phone goes off at work
I look like the biggest jerk
Total strangers wanna slap me around
When it's ringin' on the terrace
My neighbors get embarrassed
They're beggin' me to move outta town
Well, it made my wife so sick
She smashed my iPhone with a brick
But I had it fixed, and now it's just fine
It's a pain, I sure don't need it
And I probably should delete it
But for me that would be crossin' the line
'Cause I hate to waste a buck ninety nine
Hey, I paid good money for this...

Ringtone
Why did I buy this stupid ringtone?
I just can't imagine now what I was thinking at all (Really, what was I thinking?)
My friends all stare at me whenever I get a call 
Well, everybody (everybody)
Everybody (everybody)
Everybody in the world really hates my ringtone

Ringtone
Ringtone
Ahh, ooh

Chinese factory workers (they hate my ringtone)
Muslim women in burqas (really hate my ringtone)
Starvin' kids in Angola (they hate my ringtone) 
Even folks with Ebola (just hate my ringtone)
All the nuns and nannies (all the welfare mothers)
All the Pakistanis (all the Wayans brothers)
Everyone on the land, everyone on the sea
Every single person everywhere unanimously
Everybody (everybody)
Everybody (everybody)
Everybody in the whole wide world really hates my
Ringtone... ringtone... ringtone... ringtone... ringtone... ringtone...

### Post by PayPaul on 2011-09-29
Is this Flickr Central? There must be a Brock in here somewhere I'm sure.

If anyone here is also on Flickr they'd understand the reference. For the rest I'll just say that every group, every forum and every social networking site with admins always has one or two gonzo admins who take their "jobs" a bit too seriously. Brock is an admin of that Flickr Group who seem to take delight in closing and worse, deleting threads based upon his perceptions and prejudices. It happens everywhere. 

I'm a little too old for that kind of drama. I'm a little too old to care about it much. I'm here to learn something.

### Post by MG&amp;TL on 2011-10-01
I have the kind of mind that thinks (when this happens):

1. I have said something so wrong that they just thought 'what an idiot' and left.

2. I misinterpreted something, so they gave up.

3. My social skills were too appalling.

4. I was being too annoying

5. I posted too may times, so they felt swamped.

not:

1. Oh well. Threads die.

So I have a minor panic if I'm not the last poster, and end up furiously re-reading what I wrote and checking my facts. And usually end up apologising on a technicality. :( Such is life.

### Post by yetiman64 on 2011-12-04
> **Scarlet Sphere said:**
> what's a necromancer?
Someone who raises the dead, a bad thing to do to old threads on a forum and is usually frowned upon. 

For example, if you post in a thread here more that about 1 year old (since the previous last post to it) it will likely be locked by the mods for "thread necromancy" and if done often enough warnings/infractions can be given out by the mods to the poster who does so. Cheers :).

---

### Post by Scarlet Sphere on 2011-12-04
> **yetiman64 said:**
> Someone who raises the dead, a bad thing to do to old threads on a forum and is usually frowned upon.

### Post by robsoles on 2011-12-09
> **Metallion said:**
> 5 points for you. You found out where it's from but didn't give the correct answer to it yet. :)

OK, my curiosity was piqued and I had ten minutes I could kill, I researched a little.


The answer, or rather response (by nintendo), is on a follow up title: [http://www.destructoid.com/ur-mr-gay-ya-i-m-r-u--165205.phtml](http://www.destructoid.com/ur-mr-gay-ya-i-m-r-u--165205.phtml)


While I was looking for what the answer could possibly be (if it wasn't what CharlesA posted) I found most commenters accept it as intentional (at least by somebody involved in it's release, if not all parties) and some are quite certain that both are coincidental.


Looking at Mario's face on the latter (response) release and a few other details in the graphic artwork I think someone (with power) at nintendo wishes we were all gay - pity they can't go back in time and turn their ancestors gay, just occurs to me as a way of fixing it.

---

> You mean the Khajiit. Another of my favourite races actually. :) I'm all about the Khajiit, Argonians and Dunmer.

I'm playing an illusion mage. I'm always sneaking around and casting frenzy spells on every group of enemies I see. Then I watch from the shadows as they kill each other. I let others do my dirty work. If they find me, I cast calm and they leave me alone. :) I'm also pretty good with a bow. Some times I aim for the knee. :D

My favs are the High Elves, Khajit, and Daedra (:twisted:).

That sounds fun. I do a lot of sneaking with conjuration magic. Usually I summon an atronach, and then shoot from the shadows with bound bow. Or I just use fireball and burn the tar out of them! :twisted:

I've never used frenzy as a fighting tactic before. I'll have to try it.

PS- I wiped out all of Whiterun & Riverwood with my conjuration tactics.

### Post by IWantFroyo on 2012-01-20
First you need to edit the permissions.
```
chmod +x /home/robsoles/downloads/bandwagon.sh
```

Then, you will be able to run the file.
```
./home/robsoles/downloads/bandwagon.sh
```

Then, you will have successfully joined the robsoles bandwagon.

If you don't have the bandwagon.sh file, copy and paste this into your favorite text editor and save it as so.
```
echo robsoles
```

### Post by greatsirkain on 2012-12-21
Still alive. Just post something quite interesting to kick off a debate, opinions will fly, it will get heated then a mod will close it to preserve the peace. You could even word it as a question to make it look like you genuinely don't know you're being inflammatory i.e 'was 1940's Germany a hip place to be?' Another method would be to over-think a simple question and start going into great detail so everybody gets bored & just loses interest. Sometimes it's just old age that gets a thread

### Post by blnl on 2013-01-24
> **robsoles said:**
> I disagree, I've seen one or two in action :)
(both in a good way and bad.)

It depends on the definition of thread killing.

a) If you define a thread killer as a person whose post ends the discussion, then offering a solution to a problem certainly qualifies as thread killing.
Plenty of thread killers out there?
Yet I believe this is not what this thread is about.

b) If someone can kill a thread by posting rubbish, then that was a stupid thread anyway. Otherwise, rubbish would be ignored and a discussion would continue until a solution is found. 

So I still believe nobody can stop a meaningful discussion (i.e. kill a thread), without offering a solution or reaching consensus. Hence, thread killers do not exist.

---

### Post by chaceos on 2013-02-06
apokkalyps I disagree with you

blnl has a point. It is not obvious, and thread killing is a way difficult notion for someone to understand. It's like trying to convince you that the interval (0,1) has the same number of points as the whole set of real numbers 
(-inf, +inf).

Yet, I'll try to give a notion in common words:

Thread killing is an act of posting in a thread in a forum, that can be identified by the following period of time. If the following period of time that nobody else post to the same thread seems to tend to infinity, and the thread is still unsolved, then the last post's author can be charged with the crime of thread killing.

Thread killers are usually innocent mainstream computer users who declare the similarity of their problem with enthusiasm, when the thread owner himself fed up and broke his pc and lives in some village with no electricity.

But good in the act of thread killing are also young enthusiasts nerds who are keen on helping people with great posts of programming and hacking into the core of the problem.

Usually this is a standard characteristic among thread killers... enthusiasm! 

The diference between the above is the moment the thread owner decides to break his pc and go to live to a village without electricity,
In the first case the moment is unknown.
